explanation: The Sigma rule's 'selection_img' condition relies on static indicators: the executable name ending in 'rcedit-x64.exe' or 'rcedit-x86.exe', or the PE metadata 'Description' or 'Product' containing rcedit-specific strings. Because rcedit is an open-source tool, an attacker can trivially recompile it from source (or use a hex editor on the compiled binary) to remove or alter the 'Product' and 'Description' fields, and then rename the binary (e.g., to 'custom_editor.exe'). This causes 'selection_img' to fail. Since the rule's condition requires 'all of selection_*', the detection is completely bypassed.

This leverages command-line obfuscation. By inserting empty double quotes into the attribute string, the raw command line logged by Sysmon or the EDR will contain '"Original""FileName"'. The Sigma rule's 'selection_attributes' condition uses a direct string match for 'OriginalFileName'. The literal match fails due to the inserted quotes, bypassing the 'selection_attributes' requirement. Meanwhile, the Windows command-line parser (CommandLineToArgvW) often strips these inline quotes during execution, allowing the underlying tool to still process the intended attribute.

The rule uses 'all of selection_*', meaning it requires the 'selection_img' criteria to be met. By renaming the rcedit executable to something that doesn't end in 'rcedit-x64.exe' or 'rcedit-x86.exe', and ensuring the binary's internal metadata (Description/Product) is not indexed or is altered, the image-based detection is bypassed.

This bypasses the rule by renaming the file to 'r.exe' (avoiding selection_img) and potentially evading simple command-line string matching for 'OriginalFileName' if the shell or tool parses quotes within the argument string, though the primary bypass is the failure of the 'selection_img' condition.

The detection logic relies on specific filenames and metadata strings. Renaming the tool to 'legal_tool.exe' breaks the 'selection_img' requirement. Since the rule requires ALL selection blocks to be true (condition: all of selection_*), changing the filename is sufficient for a bypass.

The command uses rcedit to set an icon (--icon) rather than any '--set-*' flag, avoiding the selection_flags match. Additionally, the binary is named rcedit.exe (without the -x64/-x86 suffix) so it does not satisfy the selection_img condition based on Image ending with \rcedit-x64.exe or \rcedit-x86.exe, and assuming its Description/Product fields do not match the rule's strings, the rule will not trigger.
Uses 'resedit' instead of 'rcedit' to avoid triggering the rule's process name detection. 'resedit' is not monitored by this specific Sigma rule.
